doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_add1sp2 (mpfr/src/add1sp.c L360-L480): same-precision same-sign addition fast path for GMP_NUMB_BITS < p < 2*GMP_NUMB_BITS (= 65 <= p <= 127 on x86_64). Two-limb mantissas with sh = 128 - p in [1, 63]. The TS port uses bigint extended-precision arithmetic: order operands by exponent, compute S = (large.mant << d) + small.mant exactly, determine the result exponent from bitLen(S) (always large.exp or large.exp+1), then round S to prec bits via roundMantissa. Overflow on exp > emax delegates to mpfr_overflow. This bigint pattern matches the C version at the I/O contract level — the C's bp[0]/bp[1] / cp[0]/cp[1] decomposition and d<64 / 64<=d<128 / d>=128 case-splits all reduce to a single bigint sum.
c_signature: static int mpfr_add1sp2(mpfr_ptr a, mpfr_srcptr b, mpfr_srcptr c, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode, mpfr_prec_t p)
divergence_from_c: Static C linkage dropped (exercised via mpfr_add). C 2-limb decomposition collapsed to a single bigint sum (Law 3: faithful at the I/O contract level). Result return shape replaces in-place mutation.
refs: mpfr/src/add1sp.c L360-L480 — verbatim C reference body.
src/ops/sub1sp2.ts — sister op (sub direction) at same prec range.
